title: China’s New AI Is 6X More Efficient Than Claude
summary: A YouTube video argues that Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.7 Code and Z.ai's GLM-5.2 represent a major shift in AI development because they are open-weight coding models achieving competitive performance with closed systems. The video emphasizes that open models are now capable of supporting real developer workflows at scale.
writeup: The video covers two open-weight coding models from China, Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.7 Code and Z.ai's GLM-5.2, and argues they reach performance competitive with closed systems. It frames this as a shift in AI development: open models now support real developer workflows at scale rather than trailing proprietary alternatives. The core takeaway is that releasing model weights openly no longer means accepting a meaningful capability gap for coding and agent tasks.
